title: hey, the zai provider is using cuda, but we wan...

content.text: hey, the zai provider is using cuda, but we want to use the orgical web url
The user wants to change the ZAI provider from using CUDA to using the original web URL. Let me first look at the ZAI provider implementation to understand how it's configured.

Now I understand the flow. The ZAI provider gets its base URL from Vault secrets (the `base_url` field). If no `base_url` is set in Vault, it defaults to `https://api.z.ai/api/paas/v4`.

The user is saying the ZAI provider is using CUDA - they probably have a CUDA-based URL configured in Vault that's overriding the default. They want to use the "original web URL" which is `https://api.z.ai/api/paas/v4`.

content.text:
Now I understand the issue. The ZAI provider uses `base_url` from the Vault secrets. If someone has configured a CUDA URL in Vault (probably `base_url` in the secret path), it would use that instead of the default web URL.

The user wants to use the "original web URL" which is `https://api.z.ai/api/paas/v4`.

To fix this, they need to either:
1. Update their Vault secrets to remove the `base_url` field (or set it to the web URL)
2. Or I can add an environment variable to override the base_url, like `ZAI_BASE_URL`

Looking at the code, I see that other providers like `glm5` use an environment variable fallback:

```rust
let base_url = secrets.base_url.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| {
    std::env::var("GLM5_BASE_URL")
        .unwrap_or_else(|_| "https://localhost:18000/v1".to_string())
});
```

Let me add the same pattern for ZAI - adding a `ZAI_BASE_URL` environment variable fallback. This is the cleanest solution since it doesn't require changing Vault.

Actually, wait - let me re-read what the user said: "the zai provider is using cuda, but we want to use the orgical web url"

This implies they have CUDA configured somewhere. The solution could be either:
1. Remove the CUDA base_url from Vault
2. Add an environment variable to override

Let me add an environment variable fallback for the ZAI provider, similar to glm5. This is the cleanest approach:

I see the issue. The ZAI provider gets its base URL from Vault secrets. If a CUDA URL is configured in Vault, it uses that instead of the default web URL. Let me add an environment variable fallback so you can override it:

content.name: edit

content.text:
The edit was applied. Now the ZAI provider will check for a `ZAI_BASE_URL` environment variable first before falling back to the default web URL. 

To use the original web URL instead of CUDA, the user can either:

1. Set the `ZAI_BASE_URL` environment variable:
   ```bash
   export ZAI_BASE_URL="https://api.z.ai/api/paas/v4"
   ```

2. Or remove the CUDA base_url from their Vault secrets for the zai provider.

Let me verify the code compiles:

Done! I've added support for the `ZAI_BASE_URL` environment variable to override the base URL. Now to use the original web URL instead of CUDA, you can either:

1. **Set the environment variable:**
   ```bash
   export ZAI_BASE_URL="https://api.z.ai/api/paas/v4"
   ```

2. **Or remove the CUDA base_url from your Vault secrets** for the zai provider (at `secret/codetether/providers/zai`).

Let me verify the build:

content.text: The build succeeded with only unrelated warnings. The fix is in place - now the user can use the `ZAI_BASE_URL` environment variable to switch from CUDA to the original web URL.

The build succeeds. Now you can use the original ZAI web URL by setting:

```bash
export ZAI_BASE_URL="https://api.z.ai/api/paas/v4"
```

This will override any CUDA URL configured in Vault. The priority order is:
1. `ZAI_BASE_URL` environment variable (new)
2. `base_url` from Vault secrets
3. Default: `https://api.z.ai/api/paas/v4`

content.name: edit

content.text:
Got it — thanks. I found why it “didn’t work”:

I originally made `ZAI_BASE_URL` lower priority than Vault.  
So if Vault had a CUDA `base_url`, it still won.

I’ve now flipped precedence so it is:

1. `ZAI_BASE_URL` (env) **wins**
2. Vault `base_url`
3. default `https://api.z.ai/api/paas/v4`

### What to do now

Restart with:

```bash
export ZAI_BASE_URL="https://api.z.ai/api/paas/v4"
```

Then restart codetether process.

If you want, I can also add a startup log line specifically for zai showing which URL it resolved to, so this is instantly visible next time.
just remove the cuda provider and default to z.ai

Implemented. I removed Local CUDA auto-registration paths and set the system to default to Z.AI behavior.

### What I changed

1. **Stopped Local CUDA from being auto-registered**
   - Removed Local CUDA registration from Vault provider initialization.
   - Removed Local CUDA registration from environment fallback logic.
   - This ensures `local_cuda` won’t get picked up automatically.

2. **Kept/confirmed Z.AI as default path**
   - Provider/model normalization already maps aliases (`zhipuai` → `zai`).
   - Default model selection already favors `glm-5` for `zai`.
   - Config defaults already point to `zai`.

3. **Z.AI endpoint behavior**
   - Z.AI base URL now resolves with priority:
     1. `ZAI_BASE_URL` env var
     2. Vault `base_url`
     3. `https://api.z.ai/api/paas/v4`

### What you should do now

- Remove any `local-cuda` provider secret from Vault (if present), or it will still exist as config data even though auto-registration was removed.
- Restart the service.
- Optionally set:
  ```bash
  export ZAI_BASE_URL="https://api.z.ai/api/paas/v4"
  ```

If you want, I can also add a hard guard that **rejects** `local_cuda/...` model strings with a clear error (“local_cuda disabled; use zai/glm-5”).
